articles.title: Why thinking hard feels bad: the emotional root of deliberation
articles.titleKo: 깊이 생각하는 것이 힘든 이유: 숙고의 정서적 근원
articles.summary: Doubt is not just a lack of information; it is an unpleasant emotional state. New psychological research reveals that this negative feeling serves as a necessary alarm bell, pushing the human brain to abandon mental shortcuts and think critically.
articles.aiSummary:
This research reveals that doubt and uncertainty are not merely a lack of information, but an unpleasant emotional state. A psychology research team had participants complete various cognitive tasks and measured emotional changes when experiencing doubt. The results confirmed that when people experience doubt, their brains abandon automatic thinking patterns (heuristics) and switch to deeper analytical thinking. This suggests that negative emotions actually serve an evolutionarily important function. This finding helps us understand the role of emotions in decision-making processes. However, the study has limitations in sample size and measurements conducted in laboratory settings rather than real-world contexts.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 의심과 불확실성이 단순한 정보 부족이 아니라 불쾌한 감정 상태라는 점을 밝혔습니다. 심리학 연구팀은 참가자들에게 다양한 인지 과제를 수행하도록 한 후, 의심을 느낄 때의 감정 변화를 측정했습니다. 결과적으로 사람들이 의심을 느낄 때 뇌가 자동적인 사고 방식(휴리스틱)을 포기하고 더 깊은 사고(분석적 사고)로 전환한다는 것이 확인되었습니다. 이는 불쾌한 감정이 실제로 진화적으로 중요한 역할을 한다는 것을 시사합니다. 이 발견은 의사결정 과정에서 감정의 기능을 이해하는 데 도움이 됩니다. 다만, 연구는 제한된 표본 크기와 실험실 환경에서의 측정이라는 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: New study links watching TikTok “thirst traps” to lower relationship trust and satisfaction
articles.titleKo: 새로운 연구, TikTok '몸 자랑' 영상 시청이 연애 신뢰도와 만족도 저하와 연관 있음을 밝혀
articles.summary: Watching or liking TikTok "thirst traps" is associated with reduced romantic relationship quality. Researchers found this behavior tends to correlate with lower trust and satisfaction, especially if the creators look physically dissimilar to a person's current partner.
articles.aiSummary:
This study examined how social media consumption affects romantic relationships. Researchers investigated whether watching or liking TikTok "thirst traps" (content meant to display attractiveness) could negatively impact relationship quality. The findings revealed that higher frequency of thirst trap content consumption was significantly associated with reduced trust and satisfaction in current romantic relationships. This effect was particularly pronounced when the content creators' physical appearance differed substantially from participants' current partners. The research demonstrates that social media content consumption patterns can have meaningful effects on intimate relationships in the digital age. However, the study established only correlational relationships, and further research is needed to establish causality and test these findings across diverse populations.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 소셜 미디어 사용이 연애관계에 미치는 영향을 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 TikTok에서 "thirst trap"(매력을 드러내려는 의도의 콘텐츠)을 시청하거나 좋아요하는 행동이 연애관계의 질에 부정적인 영향을 미칠 수 있다는 가설을 검증했습니다. 연구 결과, thirst trap 콘텐츠 소비 빈도가 높을수록 현재 파트너에 대한 신뢰도와 만족도가 통계적으로 유의하게 감소했습니다. 특히 이 효과는 콘텐츠 제작자의 외모가 자신의 파트너와 크게 다를 때 더욱 두드러졌습니다. 본 연구는 디지털 시대에 소셜 미디어 콘텐츠 소비 패턴이 친밀한 관계에 실질적인 영향을 미칠 수 있음을 보여줍니다. 다만 본 연구는 상관관계만 입증했으며, 인과관계 규명과 다양한 인구집단에 대한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

##

However, the study established only correlational relationships, and further research is needed to establish causality and test these findings across diverse populations.

##
keyPoints: **주요 발견**: TikTok thirst trap 콘텐츠 시청/좋아요 빈도가 높을수록 현재 파트너에 대한 신뢰도와 만족도가 감소 / Key Finding: Higher frequency of watching/liking thirst traps correlates with reduced trust and satisfaction toward current partners
**물리적 유사성 효과**: 콘텐츠 제작자의 외모가 자신의 파트너와 다를수록 부정적 영향이 증가 / Physical Dissimilarity Effect: Negative impact intensifies when content creators' appearance differs significantly from one's partner
**관계의 질 저하**: 신뢰 감소와 만족도 하락이라는 두 가지 관계 지표의 동시적 악화 / Relationship Quality Decline: Simultaneous decrease in both trust and satisfaction metrics
**연구 한계**: 인과관계 미규명, 표본의 제한성, 추가 인구통계학적 검증 필요 / Study Limitations: Correlation only (not causation), limited sample diversity, need for further demographic validation

articles.title: Fairboard: a quantitative framework for equity assessment of healthcare models
articles.titleKo: 공정성 평가 프레임워크: 의료 모델의 형평성 정량 평가를 위한 페어보드
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.09656v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite there now being more than 1,000 FDA-authorised AI medical devices, formal equity assessments -- whether model performance is uniform across patient subgroups -- are rare. Here, we evaluate the equity of 18 open-source brain tumour segmentation models across 648 glioma patients from two independent datasets (n = 11,664 model inferences) along distinct univariate, Bayesian multivariate, spatial, and representational dimensions. We find that patient identity consistently explains more performance variance than model choice, with clinical fac
articles.aiSummary:
Despite over 1,000 FDA-authorized AI medical devices in healthcare, formal equity assessments evaluating whether model performance is uniform across different patient subgroups remain rare. Researchers evaluated the equity of 18 open-source brain tumor segmentation models across 648 glioma patients from two independent datasets, conducting a total of 11,664 model inferences. The key finding was that patient identity (demographic and clinical characteristics) consistently explained more performance variance than model choice, suggesting that the same AI model may perform differently across patient populations. The study introduces "Fairboard," a quantitative framework for multi-dimensional equity assessment (univariate, Bayesian multivariate, spatial, and representational dimensions), emphasizing that healthcare AI development must prioritize fairness and equity. However, the research is limited to glioma segmentation, and additional validation is needed to generalize findings to other diseases and medical AI applications.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
의료 분야에 1,000개 이상의 FDA 승인 AI 의료기기가 존재하지만, 서로 다른 환자 집단에서 모델의 성능이 균등한지 평가하는 공식적인 공평성 검토는 드물다는 문제에서 출발한 연구다. 연구팀은 두 개의 독립적인 데이터셋에서 수집한 648명의 뇌종양(신경교종) 환자를 대상으로 18개의 오픈소스 뇌종양 분할 모델을 평가했으며, 총 11,664번의 모델 추론 결과를 분석했다. 주요 발견은 환자의 신원(인구통계학적 특성, 임상 특성 등)이 모델 선택보다 성능 편차를 더 많이 설명한다는 것으로, 이는 같은 모델이라도 특정 환자 집단에서는 성능이 떨어질 수 있음을 시사한다. 이 연구는 의료 AI의 공평성을 다각적으로(일변량, 베이지안 다변량, 공간적, 표현적 차원) 평가할 수 있는 'Fairboard'라는 정량적 프레임워크를 제시함으로써, 향후 의료 AI 개발 시 형평성을 고려해야 함을 강조한다. 다만 이 연구는 신경교종에 국한되어 있어 다른 질환이나 의료 AI 분야에 일반화하기 위해서는 추가 검증이 필요하다.

The study introduces "Fairboard," a quantitative framework for multi-dimensional equity assessment (univariate, Bayesian multivariate, spatial, and representational dimensions), emphasizing that healthcare AI development must prioritize fairness and equity. However, the research is limited to glioma segmentation, and additional validation is needed to generalize findings to other diseases and medical AI applications.

articles.title: Deliberative Alignment is Deep, but Uncertainty Remains: Inference time safety improvement in reasoning via attribution of unsafe behavior to base model
articles.titleKo: 심사숙고적 정렬은 깊지만 불확실성이 남아있다: 기본 모델에 대한 안전하지 않은 행동의 귀속을 통한 추론 시간의 안전성 개선
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.09665v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While the wide adoption of refusal training in large language models (LLMs) has showcased improvements in model safety, recent works have highlighted shortcomings due to the shallow nature of these alignment methods. To this end, the work on Deliberative alignment proposed distilling reasoning capabilities from stronger reasoning models, thereby instilling deeper safety in LLMs. In this work, we study the impact of deliberative alignment in language models. articles.aiSummary:
This study examined the effectiveness of "Deliberative Alignment," a method to enhance the safety of large language models (LLMs). While traditional refusal training improves safety only at a superficial level, the researchers proposed a deeper approach by distilling reasoning capabilities from stronger reasoning models. The findings revealed that despite larger model sizes and stronger safety capabilities, significant uncertainties remain, and unsafe behaviors inherent to the base model architecture can influence the reasoning process. These results suggest that LLM safety alignment requires fundamental changes to model characteristics beyond surface-level control mechanisms. However, the study may be limited to specific models and datasets, requiring validation across more diverse environments to establish broader applicability.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대형언어모델(LLM)의 안전성을 강화하기 위한 '심사숙고적 정렬(Deliberative Alignment)' 방법의 효과를 조사했습니다. 기존의 거부 훈련(Refusal Training)은 표면적 수준에서만 안전성을 개선하는 한계가 있었는데, 연구진은 더 강력한 추론 모델로부터 추론 능력을 추출하여 더 깊은 수준의 안전성을 구현하는 방식을 제안했습니다. 연구 결과, 더 큰 모델 크기와 강한 안전 능력을 가진 모델에도 불확실성이 남아있으며, 모델의 기본 구조에 내재된 위험한 행동이 추론 과정에 영향을 미칠 수 있음을 발견했습니다. 이 발견은 LLM의 안전 정렬이 단순한 표면적 제어를 넘어 근본적인 모델 특성의 변화가 필요함을 시사합니다. 다만 본 연구는 특정 모델과 데이터셋에 제한되었을 가능성이 있으며, 더 다양한 환경에서의 검증이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study examined the effectiveness of "Deliberative Alignment," a method to enhance the safety of large language models (LLMs). While traditional refusal training improves safety only at a superficial level, the researchers proposed a deeper approach by distilling reasoning capabilities from stronger reasoning models. The findings revealed that despite larger model sizes and stronger safety capabilities, significant uncertainties remain, and unsafe behaviors inherent to the base model architecture can influence the reasoning process. These results suggest that LLM safety alignment requires fundamental changes to model characteristics beyond surface-level control mechanisms. However, the study may be limited to specific models and datasets, requiring validation across more diverse environments to establish broader applicability.

##
keyPoints: **기존 방법의 한계** / Limitations of Existing Methods: 거부 훈련(Refusal Training)은 표면적 수준의 안전성만 제공하며, 더 깊은 수준의 정렬이 필요함 / Traditional refusal training only provides surface-level safety improvements, requiring deeper alignment mechanisms.

**심사숙고적 정렬의 개념** / Deliberative Alignment Concept: 더 강력한 추론 모델의 능력을 추출하여 LLM에 깊은 안전성을 주입하는 방법론 / A methodology that distills reasoning capabilities from stronger models to instill deeper safety in LLMs.

**모델 크기와 안전성의 괴리** / Gap Between Model Size and Safety: 더 크고 강한 안전 능력을 가진 모델도 여전히 불확실성과 위험 행동을 포함할 수 있음 / Even larger models with stronger safety capabilities can still contain uncertainties and unsafe behaviors.

**근본적 구조의 중요성** / Importance of Fundamental Architecture: 모델의 기본 구조에 내재된 위험한 행동이 추론 과정에 영향을 미치므로, 표면적 제어보다 근본적인 변화가 필수적 / Unsafe behaviors embedded in the base model architecture influence reasoning processes, requiring fundamental changes beyond surface-level control.

articles.title: Human-like Working Memory Interference in Large Language Models
articles.titleKo: 대규모 언어 모델의 인간과 유사한 작업 기억 간섭
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.09670v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Intelligent systems must maintain and manipulate task-relevant information online to adapt to dynamic environments and changing goals. This capacity, known as working memory, is fundamental to human reasoning and intelligence. Despite having on the order of 100 billion neurons, both biological and artificial systems exhibit limitations in working memory. This raises a key question: why do large language models (LLMs) show such limitations, given that transformers have full access to prior context through attention? We find that although a two-lay
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates why large language models (LLMs) exhibit human-like working memory limitations despite their theoretical capacity to access all prior context through attention mechanisms. Working memory—the ability to maintain and manipulate task-relevant information in dynamic environments—is fundamental to human reasoning and intelligence. The researchers examined why LLMs show similar constraints to biological systems, despite having approximately 100 billion neural parameters. Initial findings reveal that even two-layer transformer models demonstrate interference effects similar to human working memory, suggesting structural processing limitations exist regardless of model size. This research has significant implications for understanding common cognitive constraints between human and artificial intelligence. However, the study's limitations include incomplete analysis (indicated by the truncated abstract) and the need to validate findings across deeper transformer architectures and more complex tasks.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대규모 언어 모델(LLM)이 인간과 유사한 작업 기억(working memory) 제약을 보이는 이유를 규명하려는 것입니다. 작업 기억은 동적인 환경에서 정보를 유지하고 조작하는 능력으로, 인간의 추론과 지능의 기초입니다. 연구진은 트랜스포머 아키텍처가 이론적으로는 모든 과거 정보에 접근할 수 있음에도 불구하고 LLM이 왜 작업 기억 제약을 경험하는지 조사했습니다. 초기 발견에 따르면 2층 트랜스포머 모델도 인간과 유사한 작업 기억 간섭 현상을 보입니다. 이는 모델의 물리적 규모(약 1000억 개 뉴런)에도 불구하고 정보 처리의 구조적 한계가 존재함을 시사합니다. 이 연구는 인간과 기계 지능의 공통적인 인지 제약을 이해하는 데 중요한 의의를 가지지만, 더 깊은 레이어를 가진 모델에서의 결과 확장 가능성을 추가로 검증해야 합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study investigates why large language models (LLMs) exhibit human-like working memory limitations despite their theoretical capacity to access all prior context through attention mechanisms. Working memory—the ability to maintain and manipulate task-relevant information in dynamic environments—is fundamental to human reasoning and intelligence. The researchers examined why LLMs show similar constraints to biological systems, despite having approximately 100 billion neural parameters. Initial findings reveal that even two-layer transformer models demonstrate interference effects similar to human working memory, suggesting structural processing limitations exist regardless of model size. This research has significant implications for understanding common cognitive constraints between human and artificial intelligence. However, the study's limitations include incomplete analysis (indicated by the truncated abstract) and the need to validate findings across deeper transformer architectures and more complex tasks.

##
keyPoints: **작업 기억의 보편적 제약** / Universal Working Memory Limitations: 인간과 LLM 모두 약 1000억 뉴런에도 불구하고 정보 유지 및 조작에 있어 동일한 제약을 보이며, 이는 신경계의 구조적 한계를 시사합니다.
**트랜스포머의 역설적 제약** / Transformer Paradox: 트랜스포머 아키텍처는 이론적으로 모든 과거 정보에 주의 메커니즘을 통해 완전히 접근 가능하지만, 실제로는 인간과 유사한 작업 기억 간섭(interference) 현상이 발생합니다.
**얕은 모델에서의 일관된 패턴** / Consistent Patterns in Shallow Models: 2층 트랜스포머 모델에서도 인간의 작업 기억 간섭이 관찰되어, 문제의 근본이 모델의 깊이보다는 근본적인 정보 처리 메커니즘에 있을 가능성을 제시합니다.
**인지 제약의 공통 원리** / Common Principles of Cognitive Constraints: 생물학적 지능과 인공 지능이 환경 적응을 위해 동적 정보 처리를 할 때 동일한 기본 제약을 공유할 수 있음을 시사하여 인지과학의 통합적 이해를 제공합니다.

articles.aiSummary:
This study applies the psychological concept of "general intelligence (g-factor)" to artificial intelligence research, marking an innovative interdisciplinary approach. The research team analyzed how Spearman's g-factor—a psychometric indicator measuring correlations between multiple abilities—manifests in AGI performance evaluations. The methodology treated different AI models as "subjects" and benchmark tests as "cognitive ability tests," applying principal component analysis (PCA) to longitudinal data across model releases. By tracking AI development patterns over time, the researchers discovered that while g-factor initially appeared strongly, it has weakened in recent models. This suggests that AI models may advance in specific domains without developing true "general intelligence." The study's significance lies in its rigorous application of psychological concepts to AI evaluation—a first of its kind. However, limitations include variations in benchmark design and constraints on temporal data availability.

articles.title: Astrocytic resource diffusion stabilizes persistent activity in neural fields
articles.titleKo: 신경장에서 별세포 자원 확산이 지속적 활동을 안정화시킨다
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.10036v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Persistent neural activity underlying working memory requires sustained synaptic transmission, yet the metabolic and neurotransmitter support provided by astrocyte networks is largely absent from spatially extended neural circuit models. We introduce a coupled astrocyte-neural field model in which synaptic efficacy is regulated by depletion and recovery of a conserved resource pool recycled and spatially redistributed through diffusively coupled astrocytes. We obtain explicit stationary bump profiles and self-consistency conditions for bump width
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates how the brain maintains persistent neural activity underlying working memory by considering the role of astrocytes—star-shaped brain cells that provide metabolic support to neurons. Traditional neural circuit models focused solely on synaptic transmission but ignored the crucial supply of nutrients and neurotransmitters from astrocytes. The research team developed a coupled astrocyte-neural field model to mathematically analyze how resource depletion and recovery regulate neural activity. The key finding demonstrates that neural bump profiles—spatially localized patterns of neural activity—are stabilized through resource diffusion and recycling within astrocyte networks. This work provides important insights into the relationship between brain energy metabolism and neural information processing. However, the current model represents a simplified mathematical framework that may not fully capture the complex physiological processes in the actual brain.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 작업 기억(단기 기억)을 유지하는 뇌의 지속적인신경 활동이 어떻게 안정화되는지를 규명하고자 했습니다. 기존의 신경회로 모델들은 시냅스 전달만 고려했지만, 실제 뇌에서는 별모양 신경교세포(아스트로사이트)가 신경세포에 필요한 영양분과 신경전달물질을 공급합니다. 연구팀은 아스트로사이트와 신경세포를 함께 모델링하여, 제한된 자원의 소모와 회복 과정이 신경 활동을 어떻게 조절하는지 수학적으로 분석했습니다. 결과적으로 신경세포 집단이 형성하는 활동 영역(범프, bump)의 폭이 자원의 확산과 순환을 통해 안정적으로 유지됨을 보였습니다. 이 연구는 뇌의 에너지 대사와 신경 정보처리의 관계를 이해하는 데 중요한 기여를 합니다. 다만, 현재 모델은 단순화된 수학적 모델이므로 실제 뇌의 복잡한 생리 현상을 완전히 반영하지 못하는 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: Relaxing in Warped Spaces: Generalized Hierarchical and Modular Dynamical Neural Network
articles.titleKo: 왜곡된 공간에서의 이완: 일반화된 계층적 모듈식 동적 신경망
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.10606v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose a dynamical neural network model with a hierarchical and modular structure. The network architecture can be derived by minimizing an energy function that is originally designed based on two kinds of neurons with quite different time constants. It has multiple subspaces that are spanned by neural parameters employed in the energy function, and adjacent subspaces are related to each other with a layered internetwork. Each internetwork further consists of a pair of a forward subnet and a backward one, and signals flowing through these sub
articles.aiSummary:
This study proposes a novel dynamical neural network model with hierarchical and modular architecture inspired by biological neural systems. The research team derived the network architecture by minimizing an energy function designed based on two types of neurons with different time constants. The proposed model features multiple subspaces spanned by neural parameters, with adjacent subspaces connected through layered internetwork connections. Each internetwork comprises a pair of forward and backward subnets through which signals flow bidirectionally. This structural design more accurately mimics brain dynamics and could enhance interpretability of neural networks. The research contributes to developing biologically-plausible AI models with improved computational efficiency. However, the model may not fully capture the complete complexity of actual neural systems in biological brains.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 생물학적 신경계의 구조에서 영감을 받아 계층적이고 모듈식 구조를 가진 새로운 동적 신경망 모델을 제안합니다. 연구팀은 서로 다른 시간 상수를 가진 두 종류의 뉴런을 기반으로 설계된 에너지 함수를 최소화하여 이 신경망 아키텍처를 도출했습니다. 제안된 모델은 신경망 매개변수로 확장된 여러 부분공간을 포함하며, 인접한 부분공간들은 계층화된 망간 연결(internetwork)로 상호 연결됩니다. 각 망간 연결은 순방향 및 역방향 서브네트워크의 쌍으로 구성되어 있으며, 이러한 구조적 특성은 뇌의 동작 방식을 더욱 정확하게 모방합니다. 이 연구는 신경망의 해석가능성을 높이고 생물학적으로 타당성 있는 AI 모델 개발에 기여할 수 있습니다. 다만, 실제 신경계의 복잡성을 완전히 포착하지 못할 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: Ketone esters show promise as a new treatment for alcohol use disorder
articles.titleKo: 케톤 에스터가 알코올 사용 장애의 새로운 치료법으로 주목받다
articles.summary: By acting as an alternative fuel for a brain starved of alcohol byproducts, a simple ketone ester drink rapidly altered brain metabolism and reduced self-reported alcohol cravings by over 30 percent in a recent study.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated ketone esters as a novel treatment for alcohol use disorder. Individuals with alcohol addiction experience abnormal brain metabolism due to dependence on alcohol metabolites; ketone esters can provide an alternative fuel source to compensate for this metabolic dysfunction. Researchers administered a ketone ester drink to participants and measured changes in brain metabolism and alcohol cravings. Results showed rapid alterations in brain metabolism following ketone ester consumption, with self-reported alcohol cravings decreasing by over 30 percent. This finding has significant implications as a potential pharmacological intervention for addiction treatment. However, limitations include small sample size, lack of long-term follow-up data, and the need to verify whether reduced cravings translate to actual reductions in drinking behavior.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 알코올 사용 장애(술 중독)의 새로운 치료법으로 케톤 에스터(ketone ester)의 효과를 검증했다. 알코올 중독자의 뇌는 알코올 대사 산물에 의존하게 되어 정상적인 에너지 대사에 문제가 생기는데, 케톤 에스터는 뇌에 대체 에너지원을 제공함으로써 이를 보완할 수 있다. 연구팀은 참가자들에게 케톤 에스터 음료를 제공한 후 뇌 대사 변화와 음주 갈망 수준을 측정했다. 결과적으로 케톤 에스터 섭취 후 뇌의 대사 과정이 빠르게 변화했으며, 자기보고식 평가에서 알코올 갈망이 30% 이상 감소했다. 이는 중독 치료의 새로운 약물 치료 가능성을 제시한다는 점에서 의의가 있다. 다만 표본 크기, 장기 추적 관찰 부재, 실제 음주 행동 감소 여부 검증 필요 등이 제한점으로 지적된다.

articles.title: Psychedelic therapy and traditional antidepressants show similar results under open-label conditions
articles.titleKo: 개방형 임상시험 조건에서 환각제 치료와 전통적 항우울제의 유사한 효과
articles.summary: Recent research is dampening the hype around psychedelic therapy. By adjusting for how clinical trials are designed, scientists found that drugs like LSD and psilocybin offer similar depression relief to traditional antidepressants, rather than vastly superior results.
articles.aiSummary: Recent research is tempering enthusiasm surrounding psychedelic therapy. By statistically adjusting for clinical trial design bias, scientists discovered that hallucinogenic drugs like LSD and psilocybin provide similar depression relief compared to traditional antidepressants, rather than dramatically superior results. Previous studies showed impressive outcomes in open-label trials where both researchers and participants knew the treatment type, likely inflated by placebo expectations. By mathematically correcting for these design biases, researchers demonstrated more objective comparisons are possible between psychedelics and conventional medications. This finding is significant for realistic evaluation of psychedelic therapy's true efficacy in clinical settings. However, the research has limitations, requiring further validation of long-term effects and effectiveness across various types of depression.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 최근 연구는 환각제 치료에 대한 과도한 기대감을 낮추고 있습니다. 임상시험의 설계 방식을 고려하여 조정한 결과, LSD와 실로사이빈 같은 환각제들이 전통적인 항우울제와 유사한 수준의 우울증 완화 효과를 보인다는 것이 밝혀졌습니다. 기존 연구들은 개방형 임상시험(연구자와 참가자 모두 약물 종류를 알고 있는 방식)에서 환각제가 매우 우수한 결과를 보였으나, 이는 심리적 기대 효과(플라시보 효과)에 의해 과장되었을 가능성이 높습니다. 과학자들은 임상시험 설계의 편향을 통계적으로 조정함으로써 더 객관적인 비교가 가능하다는 것을 입증했습니다. 이 발견은 환각제 치료의 실제 효과를 현실적으로 평가하는 데 중요한 의의가 있습니다. 다만, 연구는 장기적 효과나 다양한 우울증 유형에 대한 검증이 더 필요하다는 한계를 가지고 있습니다.

articles.title: New research links personality traits to confidence in recognizing artificial intelligence deception
articles.titleKo: 새로운 연구, 성격 특성과 인공지능 속임수 식별 신뢰도의 연관성 규명
articles.summary: A new study reveals that specific personality traits, particularly honesty and agreeableness, predict how confident young adults feel in their ability to spot deepfakes. This highlights how our psychology shapes our perceived vulnerability to digital deception.
articles.aiSummary: A new study demonstrates that specific personality traits, particularly honesty and agreeableness, predict how confident young adults feel about their ability to detect deepfakes and AI-generated deception. This research reveals how psychological characteristics shape our perceived vulnerability to digital manipulation. Researchers conducted the study with young adult participants, administering personality assessments and evaluating their self-reported confidence in detecting deepfakes. The results showed that individuals with higher honesty and agreeableness scores exhibited greater confidence in identifying AI deception, suggesting that personality traits significantly influence beliefs about one's ability to recognize online threats. These findings have implications for developing personalized digital literacy education and targeted deepfake defense strategies. However, further research is needed to clarify the relationship between self-reported confidence and actual deepfake detection ability.

articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the challenge of predicting neural activity in response to naturalistic stimuli, which is crucial for understanding brain dynamics and advancing neurotechnology applications. The research team developed a novel generative forecasting framework using autoregressive flow matching (AFM), building on recent advances in transport-based generative modeling. The approach probabilistically predicts future neural responses across multiple scales from multimodal sensory inputs including vision and auditory information. By learning the conditional distribution of future neural activity, this method enables more accurate and flexible predictions compared to traditional approaches. The research has significant implications for understanding how the brain processes information and for developing neurotechnological applications. However, predicting neural activity under naturalistic conditions remains a complex challenge, and further research is needed to account for individual differences and variability in neural responses.

articles.title: Lonely people have worse memory but don’t decline faster, study finds
articles.titleKo: 외로운 사람들은 기억력이 더 나쁘지만 더 빠르게 악화되지는 않는다는 연구 결과
articles.summary: Loneliness may quietly affect how well older adults remember things—but it might not be speeding up mental decline after all. A large European study tracking over 10,000 people for seven years found that those who felt lonelier started off with weaker memory, yet their memory didn’t deteriorate any faster than those who felt more socially connected. The findings challenge the idea that loneliness directly accelerates cognitive decline or dementia, suggesting instead that it impacts baseline brain performance.
articles.aiSummary: A large-scale European study challenges common assumptions about loneliness and cognitive decline in older adults. Researchers conducted a longitudinal study tracking over 10,000 participants over seven years to examine the relationship between loneliness and memory performance. The key finding revealed that lonely individuals started with lower baseline memory scores, but their rate of memory deterioration was similar to socially connected individuals over the study period. This contradicts the widespread belief that loneliness directly accelerates cognitive decline or dementia risk. Instead, the results suggest that loneliness primarily affects baseline cognitive performance rather than the pace of age-related decline. While this research provides valuable insights into long-term cognitive effects, limitations include the need for shorter-term analyses and investigation into other cognitive domains beyond memory.

articles.title: Your nose could detect Alzheimer’s years before symptoms begin
articles.titleKo: 당신의 코가 증상이 나타나기 수년 전에 알츠하이머병을 감지할 수 있습니다
articles.summary: Losing your sense of smell might signal Alzheimer’s far earlier than expected. Scientists found that immune cells in the brain actively destroy smell-related nerve fibers after detecting abnormal signals on their surfaces. This damage begins in early stages of the disease, well before cognitive decline. The discovery could help identify at-risk patients sooner and improve treatment timing.
articles.aiSummary:
Loss of smell could signal Alzheimer's disease far earlier than cognitive decline becomes apparent. Scientists discovered that immune cells in the brain actively destroy smell-related nerve fibers after detecting abnormal signals on their cell surfaces. This neurological damage begins in the early stages of the disease, well before memory and thinking problems emerge. The significance of this finding lies in its potential to identify at-risk patients much earlier, enabling earlier intervention and treatment timing optimization. However, the current research only establishes the mechanism of immune-mediated nerve fiber destruction, so large-scale clinical studies are necessary before this can be implemented as a diagnostic tool in clinical practice.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Romances with narcissists don’t deteriorate the way psychologists expected
articles.titleKo: 자기애성 인격장애자와의 연애는 심리학자들이 예상한 방식으로 악화되지 않는다
articles.summary: People often assume relationships with narcissists start great and crash fast. But a new six-year study shows that while hostile narcissistic traits reduce overall happiness, they don't actually speed up a romance's decline over time.
articles.aiSummary:
There is a common assumption that romantic relationships with narcissistic partners begin well and deteriorate rapidly. However, a new six-year longitudinal study tracked hundreds of dating couples to examine how narcissistic traits actually affect romantic relationships over time. The research revealed that while hostile narcissistic traits do reduce overall relationship satisfaction, they do not accelerate the decline of romantic relationships as previously expected by psychologists. This suggests that even partners with narcissistic tendencies can maintain relatively stable relationships after an initial decline. These findings challenge existing theories in relationship psychology and offer new perspectives on the sustainability of relationships with narcissistic partners. However, the study may be limited by its sample population from specific cultural backgrounds and reliance on self-report questionnaires.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
자기애적 성격장애를 가진 사람과의 연애가 급격히 악화된다는 일반적인 통념이 있다. 그러나 새로운 6년 종단연구에서 연구팀은 수백 명의 연애 중인 커플을 추적 관찰하여 자기애적 성격이 실제로 연애 관계에 어떤 영향을 미치는지 조사했다. 연구 결과, 적대적 자기애 특성이 전반적인 관계 만족도를 감소시키는 것은 맞지만, 예상과 달리 시간이 지남에 따라 연애 관계가 더 빠르게 악화되지는 않는 것으로 나타났다. 이는 자기애 성향이 있어도 관계가 초반 급락 후 안정화될 수 있음을 시사한다. 이 발견은 연애심리학의 기존 이론을 재검토하게 하며, 자기애적 파트너와의 관계 지속 가능성에 대한 새로운 관점을 제공한다. 다만 연구가 특정 문화권의 표본을 대상으로 했을 가능성과 자기보고식 설문의 한계가 있다.

articles.title: Advanced meditation techniques linked to younger brain age during sleep
articles.titleKo: 숙면 중 더 젊은 뇌 나이와 연관된 고급 명상 기법
articles.summary: People who regularly practice advanced meditation techniques exhibit brain activity patterns during sleep that appear noticeably younger than their actual age. Researchers suggest these habits might help protect against cognitive decline, though questions about exact causes remain.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated the effects of advanced meditation techniques on brain aging. Researchers analyzed brain activity during sleep in individuals who regularly practice advanced meditation and found that their brain activity patterns appeared significantly younger than their chronological age. Specifically, meditators' brain wave patterns and neural activation during sleep resembled those of younger individuals. These findings suggest that regular meditation practice may help protect against cognitive decline and age-related brain changes. However, the exact biological mechanisms by which meditation produces these protective effects remain unclear. While the research demonstrates a positive association between mental training and brain health, further studies are needed to establish causal relationships and understand the underlying neurobiological processes.

##

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…an Aerospace Engineer
articles.summary: Landing the Perseverance rover on Mars, working in clean rooms to minimize the microbial bug count, and slogging through hundreds of engineering trade-offs with Swati Mohan, an aerospace engineer at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory. In each episode of What It's Like to Be..., bestselling author Dan Heath speaks with someone about what it's like to walk in their (work) shoes. Behavioral Scientist serves as a d
articles.aiSummary:
This article analyzes a podcast episode featuring Swati Mohan, an aerospace engineer at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, from the series "What It's Like to Be..." hosted by bestselling author Dan Heath. The methodology employs direct interview format to explore the daily experiences and challenges of aerospace engineers. Key topics include the Mars Perseverance rover landing process, maintaining sterile environments in clean rooms to minimize microbial contamination, and navigating complex engineering trade-offs. Major findings highlight the critical "seven minutes of terror" during Mars landing and ongoing investigations into evidence of past life on Mars. The interview provides valuable insights for the general public to understand the real-world challenges and work processes in advanced space exploration. However, as a qualitative interview-based article rather than quantitative research, it offers limited empirical data and scientific evidence.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 기사는 NASA 제트 추진 연구소(JPL)의 항공우주 공학자 스와티 모한과의 인터뷰를 다룬 팟캐스트 에피소드 분석 기사입니다. 베스트셀러 저자 댄 히스가 진행하는 "What It's Like to Be..." 시리즈의 일환으로, 항공우주 공학자의 일상과 경험을 심층 탐구합니다. 연구 방법은 직접 인터뷰 형식으로 진행되었으며, 화성 퍼서비어런스 로버 착륙, 클린룸 환경 관리, 공학적 트레이드오프 해결 과정을 다룹니다. 주요 내용으로는 착륙 시 "7분의 공포(seven minutes of terror)"라 불리는 중요한 순간과 화성의 과거 생명 증거 탐색에 관한 논의가 포함됩니다. 이 인터뷰는 일반 대중이 첨단 우주 탐사 분야의 실제 업무와 도전 과제를 이해하는 데 도움을 줍니다. 다만 기사는 과학적 실험 데이터보다는 정성적 인터뷰 형식이므로 정량적 증거는 제한적입니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This article analyzes a podcast episode featuring Swati Mohan, an aerospace engineer at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, from the series "What It's Like to Be..." hosted by bestselling author Dan Heath. The methodology employs direct interview format to explore the daily experiences and challenges of aerospace engineers. Key topics include the Mars Perseverance rover landing process, maintaining sterile environments in clean rooms to minimize microbial contamination, and navigating complex engineering trade-offs. Major findings highlight the critical "seven minutes of terror" during Mars landing and ongoing investigations into evidence of past life on Mars. The interview provides valuable insights for the general public to understand the real-world challenges and work processes in advanced space exploration. However, as a qualitative interview-based article rather than quantitative research, it offers limited empirical data and scientific evidence.

##
keyPoints: **포인트1** / **Point 1**: 화성 착륙의 "7분의 공포(seven minutes of terror)" - 신호 전송 지연으로 인해 로버가 자동으로 착륙해야 하는 극도로 위험한 순간 / The "seven minutes of terror" during Mars landing represents the critical moment when the rover must autonomously land due to communication signal delays

**포인트2** / **Point 2**: 클린룸 환경의 중요성 - 미생물 오염을 최소화하여 화성 환경과 탐사 장비의 순결성 유지 / Maintaining clean room environments to minimize microbial contamination ensures the integrity of Mars exploration and prevents terrestrial contamination
**포인트3** / **Point 3**: 공학적 트레이드오프의 복잡성 - 수백 개의 설계 선택지 중 최적 솔루션 도출의 어려움 / Engineering trade-offs involve complex decision-making across hundreds of design alternatives to achieve optimal solutions
**포인트4** / **Point 4**: 화성의 과거 생명 증거 탐색 - 퍼서비어런스 로버의 주요 과학 목표 / Evidence of past life on Mars represents a primary scientific objective of the Perseverance rover mission

articles.title: ‘We Have Never Been Individuals’
articles.summary: Take a long, deep breath in. Now slowly let it out. Each time you inhale, you’re drawing in oxygen from the plants around you. Once in your lungs, oxygen navigates the bloodstream, where it gets exchanged for carbon dioxide. With each exhale, you fill the air with carbon dioxide, the very substance that all these plants need for photosynthesis. What we breathe out, plants breathe in. What plants breathe out, we breathe in. The air you breathe is the collective breath of other living beings. You are immersed in the world. And the world is immersed in you. Your body hosts a remarkable diversity
articles.aiSummary:
This article presents a philosophical perspective that humans are not independent individuals but exist in deep interconnection with surrounding living beings. Using respiration as an example, it explains how the oxygen humans inhale comes from plants, and the carbon dioxide we exhale is necessary for plant photosynthesis. The air we breathe is the collective breath of all surrounding organisms, and the human body contains numerous microorganisms and life forms. This supports the argument raised in ecology and psychology that "we have never been individuals" with scientific and philosophical evidence. This perspective redefines the relationship between environment and humans and promotes deeper understanding of interdependence. However, the article is limited by its focus on conceptual explanation rather than specific empirical research data or controlled experimental designs.

articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 기사는 인간이 독립적인 개체가 아니라 주변 생물들과 깊은 상호 연결 속에 존재한다는 철학적 관점을 제시한다. 호흡의 과정을 예로 들어, 인간이 들이마신 산소는 식물에서 나온 것이고 내쉬는 이산화탄소는 식물의 광합성에 필요한 물질임을 설명한다. 우리가 숨 쉬는 공기는 주변의 모든 생물들의 집단적 호흡의 산물이며, 인간의 몸은 수많은 미생물과 생명체를 포함하고 있다는 점을 강조한다. 이는 생태학과 심리학에서 제기되는 "우리는 결코 개인이 아니었다"는 주장을 뒷받침하는 과학적·철학적 근거를 제공한다. 이 관점은 환경과 인간의 관계를 재정의하고 상호의존성에 대한 깊은 이해를 촉진한다. 다만 이 기사는 개념적 설명에 중점을 두고 있어 구체적인 실증 연구 수치나 통제된 실험 설계가 부재한 한계가 있다.

##
keyPoints: **호흡을 통한 상호의존성** / Interdependence through respiration: 인간의 호흡은 식물과의 화학적 순환을 완성하는 과정으로, 개인이 고립된 존재가 아님을 보여준다 / Human respiration completes a chemical cycle with plants, demonstrating that individuals are not isolated entities.

**생물학적 연결성** / Biological connectivity: 인간의 몸은 미생물을 포함한 다양한 생명체의 집합체이며, 개인이라는 개념 자체가 환상일 수 있다 / The human body is a collective of diverse organisms including microbes, suggesting that the concept of individuality itself may be illusory.

**환경 상호침투** / Environmental interpenetration: "세상이 우리에게 침투되어 있고, 우리도 세상에 침투되어 있다"는 개념으로 경계의 모호성을 강조한다 / Emphasizes boundary ambiguity through the concept that "the world is immersed in you and you are immersed in the world."

**패러다임 전환의 필요성** / Need for paradigm shift: 개인주의 기반의 심리학과 사회 관념을 재검토하고 생태적·상호의존적 관점으로의 전환을 제안한다 / Suggests reconsidering individualism-based psychology and social concepts, proposing a shift toward ecological and interdependent perspectives.

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…a Diplomat
articles.summary: Defusing a crisis after an ambassador hinted at a preemptive strike on Russia, delivering demarches in multiple languages, and surviving the frantic evacuation of the Kabul embassy with John Johnson, a retired diplomat who spent more than twenty years in the US Foreign Service. Do diplomats still send "cables" in the 21st century? And what does "not/not" mean? [buzzsprout episode='18818060' player='true'] View the episode transcript About What It's Like to Be... In each episode of What It's Like to Be..., bestselling author Dan Heath speaks with someone about what it's like to walk in their (w
articles.aiSummary: This article features a podcast episode based on an interview with John Johnson, a retired U.S. diplomat with over 20 years of experience in the U.S. Foreign Service. The episode explores real diplomatic work and crisis management, including defusing an incident where an ambassador hinted at preemptive strikes on Russia, delivering formal protests (demarches) in multiple languages, and surviving the emergency evacuation of the Kabul embassy. The content addresses whether diplomats still use "cables" (official diplomatic communications) in the 21st century and explains diplomatic terminology and practices. Rather than providing empirical research or academic analysis, the episode offers firsthand accounts of diplomatic work to help general audiences understand the roles and responsibilities of diplomats. However, as a single case study, it may not fully represent the broader characteristics of diplomatic work across different contexts and time periods.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 이 기사는 미국 외교관의 실제 경험을 다루는 팟캐스트 에피소드로, 20년 이상 미국 외교부에서 근무한 퇴직 외교관 존 존슨과의 인터뷰를 기반으로 합니다. 주요 내용은 외교관들의 일상적 업무와 위기 상황 대응을 다루는데, 러시아에 대한 선제공격을 암시하는 사건의 해결, 다국어로 공식 항의 의사를 전달하는 과정, 그리고 카불 대사관 긴급 철수 경험 등이 포함됩니다. 이 에피소드는 21세기에도 외교관들이 여전히 "케이블"(공식 외교 통신)을 사용하는지, 그리고 외교 용어들의 의미를 설명합니다. 연구나 학술적 분석보다는 실제 외교 현장의 생생한 증언을 통해 일반인들에게 외교관의 역할과 책임을 이해하는 데 도움을 줍니다. 다만 이는 단일 사례 연구로 외교 업무의 전반적인 특성을 완전히 대표하지 못할 수 있습니다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: This article features a podcast episode based on an interview with John Johnson, a retired U.S. diplomat with over 20 years of experience in the U.S. Foreign Service. The episode explores real diplomatic work and crisis management, including defusing an incident where an ambassador hinted at preemptive strikes on Russia, delivering formal protests (demarches) in multiple languages, and surviving the emergency evacuation of the Kabul embassy. The content addresses whether diplomats still use "cables" (official diplomatic communications) in the 21st century and explains diplomatic terminology and practices. Rather than providing empirical research or academic analysis, the episode offers firsthand accounts of diplomatic work to help general audiences understand the roles and responsibilities of diplomats. However, as a single case study, it may not fully represent the broader characteristics of diplomatic work across different contexts and time periods.
keyPoints: **현장 외교의 실제 모습**: 위기 상황 해결, 다국어 공식 항의 전달, 긴급 철수 등 외교관의 다양한 역할 / **Actual Reality of Fieldwork Diplomacy**: Managing crises, delivering multilingual formal protests, and handling emergency evacuations
**현대 외교 커뮤니케이션**: 21세기에도 여전히 "케이블"을 통한 공식 외교 통신이 사용됨 / **Modern Diplomatic Communication**: Official diplomatic cables remain in use even in the 21st century
**고위험 상황 대응**: 카불 대사관 긴급 철수와 같은 생명 위협 상황에서의 외교관 경험 / **High-Risk Crisis Response**: Diplomat experiences during life-threatening situations such as the emergency evacuation from Kabul embassy

**개인 경험 기반의 이해**: 20년 경험의 외교관 증언을 통한 일반인의 외교 업무 이해 증진 / **Understanding Through Personal Experience**: Increasing public understanding of diplomatic work through testimony of an experienced diplomat with 20 years of service

articles.title: ‘Looking Backward’ to the Future
articles.summary: I first saw the light in the city of Boston in the year 1857. “What!” you say. “Eighteen fifty-seven? That is an odd slip. He means nineteen fifty-seven, of course.” I beg pardon, but there is no mistake. It was about four in the afternoon of December the 26th, one day after Christmas, in the year 1857, not 1957, that I first breathed the east wind of Boston, which, I assure the reader, was at that remote period marked by the same penetrating quality characterizing it in the present year of grace, 2000. These statements seem so absurd on their face, especially when I add that I am a young man
